Crop Name: cassava

Decision Profile
- Season urgency: high (score=0.88) Higher means shorter effective intervention windows and lower payoff from late corrections.
- Development constraint: high (score=0.68) Higher means early growth can be more thermally or photoperiod constrained; avoid overreacting to slow early biomass alone.
- Water priority: low (score=0.23) Higher means emerging water stress should more often push irrigation ahead of extra fertilizer.
- Nitrogen priority: medium (score=0.47) Higher means low nutrient status is more likely to justify nitrogen correction.
- Phosphorus priority: high (score=0.91) Higher means phosphorus limitation is more likely to matter for this crop.
- Potassium priority: medium (score=0.35) Higher means potassium correction is more likely to have value when nutrient status is low.

Use guidance
- Treat these axes as a compact decision prior rather than a crop identifier.
- Combine them with current weather, soil, and growth observations before choosing an action.
